Confirmed this remains paper-only / observation-mode work

Checked recursive log presence under mlb_model/logs to compare what historical artifacts exist versus what season data is missing

present_and_useful: shadow_recommendations.jsonl exists and is large, providing recommendation-history evidence
shadow_live.log exists and is large, providing runtime/shadow trace evidence
pipeline.log exists, giving some pipeline/runtime traceability
trades_sports.db exists and provides trade/outcome history
not_evidenced_as_populated: mlb_model/data has no demonstrated canonical current-season stats dataset in this audit
mlb_model/training has no demonstrated populated current-season training-ready dataset in this audit
mlb_model/runtime has no demonstrated canonical season data store in this audit
coverage_summary.current_season_status: Current-season MLB data coverage is not proven complete. Historical recommendation/trade logs are present, but canonical season-to-date MLB stats storage is not evidenced as complete or ready for rebuild use.

explicit_missing_ranges_or_entities: season-to-date completed MLB game stats are not evidenced as fully backfilled into a canonical dataset
team-level current-season game-by-game statistical coverage is not evidenced as complete
pitcher/bullpen current-season historical state inputs are not evidenced as stored in a rebuild-ready canonical table/file set
boxscore-level or granular per-game official stats for the current season are not evidenced as present in a standardized store
a canonical previous-day update path for ongoing season ingestion is not evidenced as existing
feature-ready normalized current-season dataset for retraining/backtesting is not evidenced as populated
priority_order_for_missing_data: 1. season-to-date completed game stats for all 2026 MLB games so far
2. per-game team and pitcher/bullpen state data needed for feature reconstruction
3. standardized canonical storage/schema for those season records
4. daily previous-day completed game ingestion to keep the dataset current
5. training-ready normalized feature dataset derived from the canonical raw season store

must_have: season-to-date MLB completed game stats in a canonical persistent store
team/game outcome records with date coverage for the full current season so far
pitcher/bullpen/game-state source fields needed to reproduce current and rebuilt features
clear canonical schema/location for raw season data
daily previous-day ingestion path so the dataset does not stall after backfill
optional_high_value: richer boxscore-level contextual fields beyond the current model minimum
additional derived historical feature caches if generated from the canonical raw store
archival convenience copies for pre-fix / post-fix segmentation
deferrable: non-essential experimental feature stores before the canonical season data foundation exists
secondary analytics artifacts that do not help rebuild or validate the core model

must_be_backfilled: standardized rebuild-ready raw dataset for 2026 coverage so far

recommended_next_enabled_task: The next justified task after this audit layer is the first code-writing data task in the rebuild plan: MLB_CURRENT_SEASON_BACKFILL_BUILD_001, but only after manager opens it with a storage/schema target. If manager wants one more non-code control step first, that should be the planned stats-foundation/storage spec artifact before the backfill build starts.
